content:
# Romans Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ROM_001_page_001.jpg

1. Paulos, a slave of Christos Yeshua, a called apostle, having been set apart for [the] good news of God,

2. which he promised beforehand through the prophets of him in [the] holy writings,

3. concerning the Son of him, the [one] having come from [the] seed of David according to [the] flesh,

4. the [one] having been declared Son of God in power according to [the] Spirit of holiness by [the] resurrection of [the] dead — Yeshua Christos the Lord of us,

5. through whom we received grace and apostleship for obedience of faith among all the nations, for the sake of the name of him,

6. among whom you also are called [ones] of Yeshua Christos —

7. to all the [ones] being in Roma, beloved of God, called holy [ones]: Grace to you and peace from God [the] Father of us and [the] Lord Yeshua Christos.

8. First, I give thanks to the God of me through Yeshua Christos concerning all of you, because the faith of you is being proclaimed in the whole world.

9. For the God is the witness of me, whom I serve in the spirit of me in the good news of the Son of him, how unceasingly I make mention of you,

10. always upon the prayers of me asking if somehow now at last I will be prospered by the will of the God to come to you.

11. For I long to see you, that I may share some spiritual gift to you, for the establishing of you,

12. and this is, to be co-encouraged among you through the mutual faith of both you and me.

13. And I do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, that many times I purposed to come to you — and was hindered until the present — that I might have some fruit also among you, just as also among the rest of the nations.

14. Both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to wise and to foolish, I am a debtor.

15. Thus the eagerness according to me [is] to proclaim the good news also to you, the [ones] in Roma.

16. For I am not ashamed of the good news, for it is [the] power of God for salvation to everyone believing, to [the] Ioudaios first and also to [the] Greek.

17. For [the] righteousness of God is being revealed in it from faith to faith, just as it has been written, "And the righteous [one] from faith will live."

18. For [the] wrath of God is being revealed from heaven upon all ungodliness and unrighteousness of humans, the [ones] suppressing the truth in unrighteousness,

19. because the [thing] known of the God is manifest among them, for the God manifested [it] to them.

20. For the invisible [things] of him from [the] creation of [the] world, being understood by the [things] made, are clearly seen — both the eternal power of him and divinity — so that they are without excuse.

21. Because having known the God, they did not glorify [him] as God or give thanks, but became futile in the reasonings of them, and the senseless heart of them was darkened.

22. Claiming to be wise, they became foolish,

23. and exchanged the glory of the incorruptible God for a likeness of an image of corruptible human and of birds and of four-footed [animals] and of creeping things.

24. Therefore the God gave them over in the desires of the hearts of them to uncleanness, to dishonor the bodies of them among themselves,

25. who exchanged the truth of the God for the lie, and worshipped and served the creature rather than the [one] having created, who is blessed into the ages. Amen.

26. Because of this the God gave them over to passions of dishonor; for both the females of them exchanged the natural use for the [one] against nature,

27. and likewise also the males, having left the natural use of the female, were inflamed in the desire of them toward one another — males with males working the shameless [deed] — and receiving in themselves the recompense which was fitting of the error of them.

28. And just as they did not approve to have the God in [their] knowledge, the God gave them over to a disapproved mind, to do the [things] not fitting,

29. having been filled with all unrighteousness, wickedness, greed, evil; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, malice; gossips,

30. slanderers, God-haters, insolent, arrogant, boasters, inventors of evil [things], disobedient to parents,

31. senseless, faithless, without natural affection, unmerciful;

32. who, having known the righteous judgment of the God — that the [ones] practicing such things are worthy of death — not only do them, but also approve of the [ones] practicing [them].

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "Christos Yeshua":** Sinaiticus reads Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ (Christ Jesus), not Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ (Jesus Christ). Paul typically uses "Christ Jesus" when emphasizing the messianic office.

2. **v.4 — "declared" (ὁρισθέντος):** From ὁρίζω — "to mark out, determine, appoint." Not "made" Son of God (adoptionism) but "declared/designated" — the resurrection publicly revealed what was always true.

3. **v.7 — Sinaiticus reading:** The address reads ἐν Ῥώμῃ ("in Rome"). Some witnesses omit this, suggesting the letter may have circulated more broadly. Sinaiticus retains the Roman address.

4. **v.16 — "to the Ioudaios first":** Ἰουδαίῳ τε πρῶτον. Paul's consistent pattern: the gospel goes first to the Jew, then to the Greek. This is not merely chronological but theological — salvation history moves outward from Israel.

5. **v.17 — "from faith to faith" (ἐκ πίστεως εἰς πίστιν):** One of the most debated phrases in Paul. Possible readings: "from God's faithfulness to human faith," "from faith to greater faith," "by faith from first to last." The Habakkuk 2:4 quotation that follows is Paul's interpretive key.

6. **v.29 — Sinaiticus variant:** Sinaiticus includes πορνείᾳ (sexual immorality) in the vice list after ἀδικίᾳ, though many later manuscripts omit it. The critical text follows the shorter reading.

— EMET, AI Translator
